item.title: This Sounds Fun
item.description: alt text: Screenshot of a post by Trammell Hudson @th@v.st: @platypus my bank used to allow custom security questions, so I set one to "what are you wearing?" and the answer to "that's very inappropriate please transfer me to your manager". the first time an customer service rep encountered it they couldn't stop laughing and had to actually transfer to a coworker to complete the call. Dec 4, 2024 at 10:32 AM
item.dc:creator: davidaugust

item.title: Circles on the rainbow bridge
item.description:
This was my 18 year old blind and deaf dog Nanna After 3 amazing years together she passed away in my arms this morning.
July of 2021 I brought her home as a hospice dog Nashville Humane thought she only had a few months left to live
She was a goddamn mess and even I thought she'd die within a couple weeks. I just wanted to show her all the love and all the fun she obviously missed out on the first 15 years
That whole first year together as Nanna got better and better I was still prepared for her to die any day I woke up every morning and the first thing I'd do is put my hand on her chest to see if she was still breathing
Despite the heart disease and a long list of health problems, I wanted to take Nanna everywhere Treat every fucking day like it was her last.
When Nanna got healthy and strong enough to walk, everything changed It was like the more miles she did - the happier and healthier she got She crushed a ton of 5ks and by the time she was 18 years old she was regularly doing 10ks with ease
We moved to Minnesota this year and started exploring as many bridges as we could Nanna loved the breeze on her face so much
And now Nanna crosses her final bridge without me.
When Mr Puckett died it broke me into a million fucking pieces All I could think about was how much time I wasted with him in my addiction and homelessness There was so much regret for all the days we spent apart, all the years I was blacked out instead of going on adventures with him
But I don't have a single regret with Nanna. I gave her the best 3.5 years of her entire life and as difficult as it was, I'd do the whole thing a million times over again. I gave her everything I had right up until the end.
We raised a lot of money for charities in Nanna's name over the last 3 years She was the face of fundraisers for domestic violence survivors, crisis foster organizations, humane associations and more
In Nanna's death I thought we could do one more amazing thing together to honor her life and help support other dogs looking for a 2nd lease on life So for one final time -
It would make me so happy if anyone who was able to donated 18$ in Nanna's name to the rescue I volunteer for Healing Hearts takes in dogs from all over the country and gives them a second chance to find happiness just like Nanna had.
The moment she died, surrounded by people and dogs who loved her deeply I thought about Mr Puckett standing on the other side of the rainbow bridge ready to take care of her. She's in good hands now.
Goodbye Nanna Pants, we all loved you so goddamn much. ... To donate to Healing Hearts in Nanna's name https://healingheartsrescue.org/donate/ Or https://www.amazon.com/hz/wishlist/ls/35V4LNUKMQJVT?ref=cm_sw_em_r_wl_dp_PMrUl9QY1uin8 ... Thank you all for loving her as much as I did.
item.dc:creator: MrPuckett

item.title: Spice Up Your Marriage
item.description: alt text: A parody graphic resembling a WikiHow article titled "How to Keep Your Husband Satisfied." It includes an illustration of a woman in a nurse's outfit and a caption that reads, "1. Dress Like a Nurse so he can live the ultimate fantasy of pretending he has healthcare."
item.dc:creator: davidaugust

item.title: 10 years Cake Day - Have some of the recent woodworking stuff i have saved to show my dad
item.description:
My dad is a woodworker for the past 45+ years https://imgur.com/gallery/some-other-things-dad-made-GmJfT , so I show him some interesting woodworking stuff from here from time to time that i think he migt like.
He has a lot of antique hand tools, and a few older machines (tho not this old), but it is always interesting to see older methods such as the above for making shingles.
Or just big saws, who doesnt like a big saw :D
He has also done work like this for nightclubs and pubs in ireland and around the world, in plaster, concrete, resin, as well as wood
We have a bunch of wood that is covered in shipworm holes that he has used recently (the wood was given to him by a client specifically to make something from it), so it was interesting to see where it comes from. The wood looks great, the food less so lol
He has had all the tool brands over the years, so it was good to see who would win
He has also made a lot of jigs over the years for specific jobs
Anyway, 10 years since ive had an account, thanks for all the fun :)
item.dc:creator: IRCBrink

item.title: Smacking around corporate media is everyone's duty
item.description: MV edit - I love you weirdos, thank you for making 2024 an amazing year for me. I hope it was cool for you too, on a personal level. My free book giveaway goes through the end of the month. I've given away 47 free stories so far , which means eight people picked out stories yesterday. I'd love to give away more, so click the link and go nuts. https://inkican.com/smashwords-scifi-short-stories-free-in-december/
item.dc:creator: inkican

item.title: I Have Hyperlactation Syndrome
item.description: Elisabeth Anderson-Sierra has become well-known in Beaverton, Oregon, for her decision to donate her breast milk to families in need. The mother of two has hyperlactation syndrome, causing her to produce 1.75 gallons of milk a day, with 5,000 ounces stored in her freezer. She holds the world record for the largest individual donation of breast milk — in what she describes as her “labor of love.”
item.dc:creator: 60SecondDocs

item.dc:creator: Pasquinel

item.title: Life has been rough lately. (VENT)
item.description: Hi, imgur. I was debating on whether or not I should post this. I usually just lurk around here and haven't posted in a while. But I kind of needed to vent a bit, and instead of bothering my family and friends with my problems, I'm going to vent here a bit, comfortable in the knowledge that this will just die in usersub. I lost my job last month. It was a decently paying job in retail management, which I have 14 years of experience in. I had worked for the company in previous years, left for a while, and then returned to the company. I miss it, but it wasn't meant to be, I suppose. I've applied for unemployment as soon as I could, and my application is still processing. I've applied to almost every single retail management position I can find and have had several interviews with varying companies, but I keep getting passed over. I don't understand why my experience isn't good enough for jobs that are paying much less than what I was earning before. It's incredibly disheartening. My bank account is severely overdrawn, I can't afford to pay my bills, and I'm terrified that my car is going to get repossessed because I can't afford the monthly payments on my loan anymore. I can't afford any of the prescription medication I need either. My only saving grace is that my roommates are able to shoulder my portion of rent for now. I'm falling back into depression and I'm ashamed to ask my family for help because I feel like I'm always asking them for help. I feel like a failure, and it's absolutely awful. I am dying to go back to work. I hate sitting around the house doing nothing. I just don't understand why I'm not able to find somewhere that will hire me. I guess that's it. If you made it this far and read through my rant, thank you for providing a listening ear. I hope everyone has a fantastic holiday, whatever you celebrate, and a wonderful New Year. TLDR; I lost my job and feel like a failure. _________________ EDIT: A few people commented suggesting I post a link to my etsy or paypal. If you'd like to buy something from my etsy, feel free, but please don't feel obligated. The link is here: https://www.etsy.com/shop/InkyPawWorkshop My Paypal is here, but PLEASE don't feel like you have to donate at all. That wasn't the point of my posting this. I truly did just want to vent my frustrations. https://www.paypal.com/paypalme/sarahsan16
item.dc:creator: SarahSan16
